reasoning: The Fed officially ended Quantitative Tightening (QT) in December 2025 and introduced Reserve Management Purchases (RMPs) to expand the balance sheet by roughly $40bn per month (approx. $10bn per week) to ensure ample banking reserves. Recent FRED data shows this upward trajectory strongly taking hold, with total assets growing from 6.53M in early December to 6.61M by late February 2026. For the March 8 resolution date (reflecting the March 4 weekly observation), the short-term structural inflow of RMPs competes with typical weekly noise (+/- $10-15bn seen in recent mid-month fluctuations), meaning there is a high likelihood (0.76) that the balance sheet will be higher than the Feb 25 baseline. However, for the March 31 resolution date (reflecting the March 25 observation), following the strict system instruction that uncertainty must increase and probabilities shift closer to 0.5 for more distant horizons, I have modulated the forecast downwards to 0.65. This theoretically accommodates exogenous risks—like an unexpected policy pivot or a halt in RMPs at the mid-March FOMC meeting—that could introduce structural drag beyond the weekly noise.
